_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
This document covers Regulation (EU) 2023/2405 (ReFuelEU Aviation), which entered into force on 1 January 2025 and imposes binding sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) blending mandates on aviation fuel suppliers and aircraft operators at EU airports. The regulation is part of the EU's Fit for 55 legislative package.

This brief is anchored to a freight forwarding operation for which air is the primary transport mode, with active trade lanes covering Americas, Europe, and Asia, and cargo verticals including live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian freight. The workspace does not hold an aircraft operator certificate and does not supply aviation fuel; its exposure is indirect, through airline surcharge pass-through as a buyer of air cargo capacity.

**Conventions used in this document:**
- "Legal Confirmation Required" flags items requiring review by qualified legal counsel before action.
- Inline citations appear at the end of each subsection in the format: *Source: [Title], [Issuing Body], [Date]. [URL].*
- Cause-and-effect chains are structured: cause → mechanical consequence → effect on the workspace.
- The workspace is referenced generically by role and operational profile, never by name.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, Official Journal of the European Union, 31 October 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng*

---
